summarization.a: The figure displays the ion energy and angular distribution (IEAD) during the first step (passivation). The color gradient represents relative intensity of ions arriving at that specific energy and angle.
summarization.b: The figure displays the ion energy and angular distribution (IEAD) during the second step (etching). The color gradient represents relative intensity of ions arriving at that specific energy and angle.

b.question: What is the angular distribution of the ions during the etching step?
At approximately what energy do most ions arrive during the etching step?

a.question: What is the angular distribution of low-energy ions during the passivation step?
Are the most ions arriving the wafer low-energy or high energy? What is the reason to choose these energy range?
a.answer: The most ions arriving at the wafer in are low-energy (with energies below 30 eV). This energy range was chosen to promote passivation.Higher energies would cause sputtering of the surface.
